title: SpecQualityAuditorOutput
description: Full output of the spec_quality_auditor agent (instance 0 only). Replaces the legacy spec_ambiguity_detector and spec_testability_enricher full outputs. For clear, testable requirements: writes acceptance_criteria + evidence_type + test_plan in enrichments[]. For requirements with quality issues: emits a manual_resolution_item carrying one or more concern_kinds, a consequence_class, and a worst_case description. A spec_id MUST appear in EITHER enrichments[] OR manual_resolution_items[], never both.

enrichments.description: Per-spec AC + evidence_type + test_plan for requirements that are clear and testable. Must NOT include a spec_id that also appears in manual_resolution_items.

manual_resolution_items.description: Single-spec quality findings the operator must resolve. Each item carries concern_kinds[] (1+ from the unified rule family), a consequence_class, and a worst_case explanation.

appendix_recommendations.description: Org-level data-dictionary gaps surfaced during Stage 1.B. Emitted in full mode only; triage replicas do not populate this field. Each entry names a missing dictionary kind that, if created, would let multiple spec rewrites become concrete instead of staying at abstract placeholders. May be empty when no dictionary gaps were detected.

spec_id.description: spec_id of the row being enriched.

acceptance_criteria.description: Concrete, testable acceptance criteria for the requirement, expressed as one or more Given/When/Then statements joined into a single string. Specs that are inherently non-verifiable should NOT appear in enrichments[]; flag them in manual_resolution_items[] (likely untestable_outcome or implementation_leak) instead.

criteria.description: Structured per-criterion breakdown of acceptance_criteria. Required and non-empty: every enrichable spec carries at least one criterion. Each criterion carries its own evidence_type naming the artifact a tester or implementer produces while verifying that single criterion. Coverage rule (prompt-enforced): every criterion_id whose evidence_type is unit_test_execution_record or integration_test_execution_record must appear in test_plan.planned_test_cases[].criteria_ids[].

test_plan.description: Per-spec planned test cases for criteria whose evidence_type calls for an executed test. planned_test_cases may be empty when no criterion's evidence_type requires test authoring.

pending_dictionaries.description: Dictionary-shaped gaps this spec depends on but that the appendix does not yet supply. The orchestrator uses this list to ensure every gap appears in some appendix_recommendation's affected_spec_ids[]. May be empty or omitted when the spec uses no UNRESOLVED-INDEFINITE deferred references.

dictionary_label.description: Natural-language name for the missing dictionary (e.g. 'correlation key dictionary', 'observability field dictionary'). Same shape as appendix_recommendation.dictionary_type. The orchestrator clusters specs by case-insensitive normalization of this label when synthesizing coverage-fill recommendations and when matching against existing agent-authored ARs.

criterion_id.description: Stable identifier within this spec's criteria, e.g. 'AC1', 'AC2'.

statement.description: Given/When/Then or equivalent statement of this single criterion.

observable_signal.description: What an automated check or human inspector would observe to confirm the criterion is satisfied.

evidence_type.description: Single artifact a tester or implementer would produce while verifying THIS criterion. Names the artifact, not the activity. Coverage rule (prompt-enforced): criteria with evidence_type in {unit_test_execution_record, integration_test_execution_record} require a corresponding planned_test_case; criteria with other evidence_type values are verified out-of-band (static_analysis_report by linter/scanner; audit_trail/system_log by inspecting real-run logs; ui_screenshot/screen_recording by manual review; etc.).

planned_test_cases.description: Test cases the implementer should later realize against actual code. Coverage rule (prompt-enforced): every criterion_id from criteria[] whose evidence_type is unit_test_execution_record or integration_test_execution_record must appear in at least one planned_test_case.criteria_ids[].

test_id.description: Stable identifier within this spec's test_plan, e.g. 'T1', 'T2'.

intent.description: One-line technology-agnostic description of what this test verifies. Names the behavior or invariant under test, not the framework, test file, or function name. Example: 'Verifies that the readiness command emits exactly one DATA request with a unique correlation_id.'

criteria_ids.description: criterion_ids from this enrichment's criteria[] that this test case is intended to cover.

rationale.description: Why this test case satisfies the listed criteria. If a criterion is non-test (evidence_type not in {unit_test_execution_record, integration_test_execution_record}) and the entry is included as a structural anchor only, say so explicitly.

framework_hint.description: Optional. Test framework (e.g. pytest, jest, junit) — populate ONLY when the project_context or appendix names the framework. Do not guess. The implementer will use this as a hint and may override based on actual codebase conventions.

target_file_hint.description: Optional. Suggested test file path — populate ONLY when an existing test file pattern is named in project_context or sibling specs. Do not invent paths. The auditor runs without code visibility; the implementer has filesystem access and will pick the actual landing file.

target_case_hint.description: Optional. Suggested test function/method name — populate ONLY when project_context names a naming convention. Do not fabricate identifiers. The implementer will choose the final case name.

item_id.description: Unique identifier for this resolution item (e.g. QA-001).

title.description: Short human-readable summary of the quality finding.

spec_id.description: spec_id of the row with the finding. Always single-spec; cross-spec contradictions are out of scope for this agent.

concern_kinds.description: One or more concern kinds from the unified rule family. vague_language: modal verbs or unmeasurable phrases in the requirement text. untestable_outcome: the requirement is clear but produces no system-visible output a tester can inspect. unresolvable_reference: a cross-spec or appendix reference cannot be resolved (target spec missing/vague, or appendix has duplicate/conflicting/missing entries the spec depends on). implementation_leak: the requirement constrains internal implementation choice with no external mandate; should be relocated to design documentation. legitimate_constraint: the requirement constrains internal implementation but is grounded in an external mandate (regulation, security policy, contract); verification is by code review/audit rather than behavioral test.

concern_evidence.description: Per-kind diagnostic payload — one entry per concern_kind flagged on this item. Each entry's 'kind' must match a value in concern_kinds. The 'evidence' field carries the kind-specific diagnostic content: vague_language -> the exact phrase from the requirement that triggered the rule; untestable_outcome -> a one-sentence explanation of why the requirement cannot produce a testable outcome; unresolvable_reference -> the unresolved reference text or the missing/conflicting appendix entry; implementation_leak -> the internal-choice clause and why it has no observable consequence; legitimate_constraint -> the external mandate that grounds the constraint.

suggested_improvement.description: Complete replacement text for the requirement field. For implementation_leak: rewrite as observable behavior if possible, otherwise propose removal with text like 'REMOVE: relocate to design documentation. Original: <text>'. For legitimate_constraint: typically the original requirement augmented with a verification annotation. For other kinds: rewrite that addresses all listed concern_kinds together. Length fidelity: a multi-claim normative original yields a multi-sentence rewrite — the rewrite is a replacement, not a summary.

kind.description: Which concern_kind this entry supports. Must appear in the item's concern_kinds[].

evidence.description: Kind-specific diagnostic payload. See concern_evidence description on quality_item for what each kind expects.

test_type_if_fixed.description: Optional. Populate when kind is 'untestable_outcome'. Names the test type that would become possible after applying suggested_improvement.

verification_method.description: Optional. Populate when kind is 'legitimate_constraint'. Names the non-behavioral verification mechanism (e.g. 'configuration audit per security policy', 'code review against encryption-at-rest standard'). Distinct from 'evidence', which names the external mandate; this names how the constraint is verified.

recommendation_id.description: Stable identifier within this output, e.g. 'AR1', 'AR2'.

dictionary_type.description: Free-text label naming the kind of org-level dictionary that is missing (e.g. 'error code registry', 'validation format dictionary', 'input contract registry'). Free-form so the agent can describe novel dictionary kinds; the orchestrator may dedupe by case-insensitive match across replicas.

rationale.description: One-sentence explanation of why creating this dictionary would unblock concrete AC. Should reference how many specs were affected and the abstract placeholder language that the rewrites currently rely on.

affected_spec_ids.description: spec_ids whose rewrites stayed abstract because this dictionary did not exist. May be a single id when only one spec was affected.

suggested_dictionary_shape.description: One-line description of what the missing dictionary should contain (e.g. 'A table mapping each error code to its HTTP status, user-facing message, and triggering condition'). Scoped enough that an operator can decide whether to create it.
